examples.title: LDAP Search Operation
examples.description: Example of searching for users in a specific organizational unit
examples.explanation: This LDAP search command searches for all entries with objectClass=person in the Users OU. The -x flag uses simple authentication, -h specifies the host, and -b specifies the search base.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is entries searched
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is matching entries

examples.title: LDAP Add Entry
examples.description: Adding a new user entry to the directory
examples.code:
dn: uid=jsmith,ou=Users,dc=example,dc=com
objectClass: inetOrgPerson
objectClass: posixAccount
objectClass: shadowAccount
uid: jsmith
cn: John Smith
sn: Smith
givenName: John
mail: jsmith@example.com
uidNumber: 1000
gidNumber: 1000
homeDirectory: /home/jsmith
examples.explanation: This LDIF (LDAP Data Interchange Format) file defines a new user entry with required attributes including object classes and personal details.

examples.title: LDAP Bind and Authentication Process
examples.code:
FUNCTION authenticateUser(username, password):
 // Step 1: Establish connection
 connection = ldap_connect(ldap_server, port=389)
 
 // Step 2: Bind with credentials
 bind_result = ldap_bind(connection, username, password)
 
 IF bind_result == SUCCESS THEN
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
 
 // Step 3: Cleanup
 ldap_unbind(connection)
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows the basic authentication flow in LDAP: connect to server, bind with user credentials, check result, and clean up connection.

examples.title: Kerberos Authentication Flow
examples.explanation: Kerberos uses a ticket-granting system: client gets a TGT from KDC, then uses TGT to request specific service tickets without re-entering password.

examples.title: LDAP Modify Operation
examples.description: Updating user attributes in the directory
examples.code:
dn: uid=jsmith,ou=Users,dc=example,dc=com
changetype: modify
replace: mail
mail: john.newemail@example.com
-
add: telephoneNumber
telephoneNumber: +1-555-0123
-
delete: description
examples.explanation: This LDIF demonstrates modifying an entry: replacing an attribute value, adding a new attribute, and deleting an existing attribute.
